narration: Create a class A with a public void show method that prints 'hi' 10 times using a loop.
Create a class B with a public void show method that prints 'hello' 10 times using a loop.
In main, instantiate objects of class A and class B.
Compile and run the code to observe sequential execution.
Extend class A and class B to inherit from Thread class.
Call the start method on both thread objects to begin execution.
Increase loop iterations to 100 to observe parallel execution.
Explain that the OS scheduler manages thread execution across CPU cores.
